contentBlocks.original:
सम्पूर्ण स्तोत्रम्
सौराष्ट्रदेशे विशदेऽतिरम्ये ज्योतिर्मयं चन्द्रकलावतंसम् ।
भक्तप्रदानाय कृपावतीर्णं तं सोमनाथं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sampūrṇa stōtram
saurāṣṭradēśē viśadē'tiramyē jyōtirmayaṃ chandrakaḻāvataṃsam ।
bhaktapradānāya kṛpāvatīrṇaṃ taṃ sōmanāthaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the gloriously beautiful land of Saurashtra, the luminous one adorned with the crescent moon, who has descended with compassion to bless devotees — I salute that Somanatha, the first among Jyotirlingas.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशैलशृङ्गे विविधप्रसङ्गे शेषाद्रिशृङ्गेऽपि सदा वसन्तम् ।
तमर्जुनं मल्लिकपूर्वमेनं नमामि संसारसमुद्रसेतुम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśailaśṛṅgē vividhaprasaṅgē śēṣādriśṛṅgē'pi sadā vasantam ।
tamarjunaṃ mallikapūrvamēnaṃ namāmi saṃsārasamudrasētum ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Eternally dwelling on the peak of Shrishaila, in its many sacred dimensions, and on the peak of Seshachala as well — I bow to that Mallikarjuna, the bridge across the ocean of samsara.

contentBlocks.original:
अवन्तिकायां विहितावतारं मुक्तिप्रदानाय च सज्जनानाम् ।
अकालमृत्योः परिरक्षणार्थं वन्दे महाकालमहासुरेशम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avantikāyāṃ vihitāvatāraṃ muktipradānāya cha sajjanānām ।
akālamṛtyōḥ parirakṣaṇārthaṃ vandē mahākālamahāsurēśam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having taken avatara at Ujjain (Avantika) to grant liberation to righteous souls and to protect against untimely death — I worship the great Mahakala, the supreme Lord of all.

contentBlocks.original:
कावेरिकानर्मदयोः पवित्रे समागमे सज्जनतारणाय ।
सदैव मान्धातृपुरे वसन्तं ओङ्कारमीशं शिवमेकमीडे ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāvērikānarmadayōḥ pavitrē samāgamē sajjanatāraṇāya ।
sadaiva māndhātṛpurē vasantaṃ ōṅkāramīśaṃ śivamēkamīḍē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the sacred confluence of the Kaveri and Narmada rivers, for the liberation of the righteous, ever dwelling in the city of Mandhata — I praise Omkareshvara, the one supreme Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
पूर्वोत्तरे प्रज्वलिकानिधाने सदा वसं तं गिरिजासमेतम् ।
सुरासुराराधितपादपद्मं श्रीवैद्यनाथं तमहं नमामि ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūrvōttarē prajvalikānidhānē sadā vasaṃ taṃ girijāsamētam ।
surāsurārādhitapādapadmaṃ śrīvaidyanāthaṃ tamahaṃ namāmi ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the northeastern sacred region where the divine flame is enshrined, always dwelling together with Girija (Parvati) — with lotus feet worshipped by both gods and demons — I bow to Sri Vaidyanatha.

contentBlocks.original:
यं डाकिनिशाकिनिकासमाजे निषेव्यमाणं पिशिताशनैश्च ।
सदैव भीमादिपदप्रसिद्धं तं शङ्करं भक्तहितं नमामि ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaṃ ḍākiniśākinikāsamājē niṣēvyamāṇaṃ piśitāśanaiścha ।
sadaiva bhīmādipadaprasiddhaṃ taṃ śaṅkaraṃ bhaktahitaṃ namāmi ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ever worshipped by the assembly of Dakinis and Shakinis (tantric adepts) and by flesh-eating beings — ever famous by the name Bhima and other epithets — I bow to Shankara, the benefactor of devotees (Bhimashankar).

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीताम्रपर्णीजलराशियोगे निबध्य सेतुं विशिखैरसङ्ख्यैः ।
श्रीरामचन्द्रेण समर्पितं तं रामेश्वराख्यं नियतं नमामि ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrītāmraparṇījalarāśiyōgē nibadhya sētuṃ viśikhairasaṅkhyaiḥ ।
śrīrāmachandrēṇa samarpitaṃ taṃ rāmēśvarākhyaṃ niyataṃ namāmi ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Jyotirlinga offered and installed by Sri Ramachandra, who built the sacred bridge across the southern ocean near the Tamraparni river with countless arrows — I bow to that Rameshvara with devotion.

contentBlocks.original:
याम्ये सदङ्गे नगरेऽतिरम्ये विभूषिताङ्गं विविधैश्च भोगैः ।
सद्भक्तिमुक्तिप्रदमीशमेकं श्रीनागनाथं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāmyē sadaṅgē nagarē'tiramyē vibhūṣitāṅgaṃ vividhaiścha bhōgaiḥ ।
sadbhaktimuktipradamīśamēkaṃ śrīnāganāthaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the supremely beautiful city to the south (Daruka forest), with body adorned by various offerings — the one Ishvara who grants both devotion and liberation — I take refuge in Sri Nageshvara.

contentBlocks.original:
सानन्दमानन्दवने वसन्तं आनन्दकन्दं हतपापबृन्दम् ।
वाराणसीनाथमनाथनाथं श्रीविश्वनाथं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sānandamānandavanē vasantaṃ ānandakandaṃ hatapāpabṛndam ।
vārāṇasīnāthamanāthanāthaṃ śrīviśvanāthaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dwelling in Anandavana (the grove of bliss, Kashi), embodiment of bliss, root of all joy, destroyer of hosts of sins — the Lord of Varanasi, the Lord of the lordless — I take refuge in Sri Vishvanatha.

contentBlocks.original:
सह्याद्रिशीर्षे विमले वसन्तं गोदावरितीरपवित्रदेशे ।
यद्दर्शनात् पातकं पाशु नाशं प्रयाति तं त्र्यम्बकमीशमीडे ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sahyādriśīrṣē vimalē vasantaṃ gōdāvaritīrapavitradēśē ।
yaddarśanāt pātakaṃ pāśu nāśaṃ prayāti taṃ tryambakamīśamīḍē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dwelling purely on the crest of the Sahyadri mountains, in the holy region on the banks of the Godavari — by whose darshan sins are instantly destroyed — I praise that Tryambaka Ishvara (Trimbakeshvara).

contentBlocks.original:
महाद्रिपार्श्वे च तटे रमन्तं सम्पूज्यमानं सततं मुनीन्द्रैः ।
सुरासुरैर्यक्ष महोरगाढ्यैः केदारमीशं शिवमेकमीडे ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahādripārśvē cha taṭē ramantaṃ sampūjyamānaṃ satataṃ munīndraiḥ ।
surāsurairyakṣa mahōragāḍhyaiḥ kēdāramīśaṃ śivamēkamīḍē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Delighting on the banks of the great Himalayas, ever worshipped by great sages, surrounded by gods, demons, yakshas, and great serpents — I seek refuge in the one auspicious Kedara Ishvara.

contentBlocks.original:
इलापुरे रम्यविशालकेऽस्मिन् समुल्लसन्तं च जगद्वरेण्यम् ।
वन्दे महोदारतरस्वभावं घृष्णेश्वराख्यं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ilāpurē ramyaviśālakē'smin samullasantaṃ cha jagadvarēṇyam ।
vandē mahōdāratarasvabhāvaṃ ghṛṣṇēśvarākhyaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shining brilliantly in the great and beautiful city of Ilapura (Verul/Ellora), the most revered in all the worlds — I bow to that Ghrishneshvara of supremely magnanimous nature, seeking his refuge.
